person.name: Christian Cheyne
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography
Parentage«/b»
Christian Cheyne was the daughter of the Laird of Straloch and an unknown mother, although the names of her parents are unknown. She was the product of a Norman-Scotch family who had come to Scotland during the Conquest and settled in Aberdeenshire. Christian's date of birth has been estimated as occurring around 1279, as she likely married circa 1300 (see marriage for explanation), with the assumption that she may have been about 21 years old at the time of her marriage.

«b»Marriage«/b»
Christian Cheyne is said, by Maitland, Balfour Paul, and others, to have married Alexander, son of Alexander Seton. According to Sir Bruce Gordon Seton the marriage took place circa 1311 in Aberdeenshire, however, Christian's youngest son, John, is known to have married and had a son, Alexander, who married in 1346. If Christian Cheyne and Alexander married in 1311 and son John was born circa 1319 (as the fourth child), John's son, Alexander, may have been born around 1325, given his 1346 marriage. This would place John's age at around six at the time of his son's birth. It is more likely, therefore, that the marriage of Christian and Alexander occurred before 1300.

«b»Christian and Alexander had four known children:«/b»
1.) Thomas, hanged by command of Edward III, while being held hostage during the siege of Berwick in 1333. His marital status is unknown.

2.) William, drowned in the Tweed during the siege of Berwick, while setting fire to enemy ships. His marital status is likewise unknown.

3.) Alexander, was likely the Sir Alexander Seton who was sent to Newcastle-on-Tyne in 1323 and referenced by Robert I as "his bachelor." Alexander lost his life at Kinghorn in Fife, where he opposed the landing of Edward Balliol's ship on 6 August 1332. According to Bruce Gordon Seton, Alexander married Jean Haliburton, daughter of Sir Thomas of Dirleton, but no other reviewed sources corroborate this claim.

4.) John, died during his father's lifetime after 1327, but before 1346, by which time his son Alexander is referenced as Lord of that Ilk. While Maitland contended that John married Elizabeth Ramsay, heiress of Parbroth, Balfour Paul claimed that as of 1911 there were no extant records of this marriage. John's son and heir, Alexander Seton (who may have been born around 1325), was said to have married Margaret de Ruthven in 1346. Alexander appeared to have died shortly after his marriage and according to Balfour Paul may have died on 17 October 1346 at the Battle of Durham. He seems to have died without issue. John may also have had a daughter, Margaret Seton, who was the known granddaughter of Sir Alexander Seton, Governor of Berwick and Christian Cheyne. Her parentage, however, is uncertain and she may instead have been John's niece (see research note). She married Alan de Wyntoun in 1347, following her forced abduction by him.

«b»Notable Events«/b»
Shortly after 6 August 1332, Christian lost her son Alexander who died at Kinghorn in Fife. At Berwick on the date of surrender on 20 July 1333, less than a year after losing Alexander, Christian was present with her husband and witnessed the execution of her son, Thomas. Her son William was also lost in this seige. Her son John died between the years 1327 and 1346, so it is possible her youngest son, too, may have died in her lifetime. To say that this woman was acquainted with grief is an understatement.

«b»Death«/b»
Although no primary or secondary sources were located that provide a date of death for Christian, it is known that her husband, Alexander, became a member of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem around 1341, where he was required to divest of his property and take the monastic vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience. It is unknown to whom Alexander granted his principal land holdings, but his vows of poverty and chastity suggest the possibility that his wife may have been deceased by this time.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Christian Cheyne於大約1292年出生在蘇格蘭培爾斯特拉洛時，她的父親Freskin Francis Cheyne34歲，母親Isabel Comyn19歲。她和Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick至少有8個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於大約1341年在她的家鄉享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Christian Cheyne于大约1292年出生在苏格兰培爾斯特拉洛时，她的父亲Freskin Francis Cheyne34岁，母亲Isabel Comyn19岁。她和Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick至少有8个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于大约1341年在她的家乡享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cheyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (northeastern Scotland and Shetland): shortened form of   Chaney  , of Norman origin.

variants.name: Chaney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any one of several places in Normandy, Picardy, or Artois (France) named (Le) Quesnay or Quesnoy, or elsewhere in northern France named (Le or La) Chesnay or Chesnoy. The placenames all derive from Old French chesnai or its Normanno-Picard equivalent quesnai ‘oak grove’ (medieval Latin casnetum).
French: variant of Chanet, a habitational name from any of various places called Chanet or Le Chanet, from Latin canna ‘reed’ + the suffix -etum denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Christian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

variants.name: Christianne
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

en.lifeSummary: When Christian Cheyne was born about 1292, in Straloch, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, Freskin Francis Cheyne, was 34 and her mother, Isabel Comyn, was 19. She had at least 8 sons and 1 daughter with Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick. She died about 1341, in her hometown, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Christian Cheyne est née environ 1292, à Straloch, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, Freskin Francis Cheyne, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabel Comyn, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille avec Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick. Elle est décédée environ 1341, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Christian Cheyne um 1292 in Straloch, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Freskin Francis Cheyne 34 und ihre Mutter Isabel Comyn 19. Sie hatte mit Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1341 im Alter von 51 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Christian Cheyne è nata il circa 1292 nel Straloch, Perthshire, Scotland, sua padre, Freskin Francis Cheyne, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Isabel Comyn, aveva 19 anni. Lei avuto almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia con Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick. Morì circa 1341, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Christian Cheyneが約1292年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアストラロチで生まれた時，父Freskin Francis Cheyneは34歳，母Isabel Comynは19歳であった。彼女はAlexander Seton Governor of Berwickとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1341年に故郷で51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Christian Cheyne(이)가 대략 1292년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 스트라럭에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Freskin Francis Cheyne(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Comyn(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1341년 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Christian Cheyne родилась около 1292 г. в местности стралоч, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Freskin Francis Cheyne было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Comyn, 19. У неё было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь от Alexander Seton Governor of Berwick. Она умерла около 1341 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 51 год.
